title: Search an element in an array

content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN and an element XXX. Your task is to find whether the array AAA contains the element XXX or not.

**Function Declaration****Function Name**solvesolvesolve – This function checks whether a given element **X** is present in the array **A**.

**Parameters**
NNN : An integer representing the number of elements in the array.
XXX : An integer representing the element to be searched.
AAA : A list/array of integers of length **N**, representing the input array.
**Return Value**

Returns a **string**:



`"YES"` if the element XXX exists in the array AAA.
`"NO"` if the element XXX is not present in the array.



Input Format

The first line contains two space-separated integers NNN and XXX — the size of array and the element to be searched.
The second line contains all the elements of array AAA


Output Format
Output "YES" if the element XXX is present in AAA, otherwise output "NO".
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains two space‑separated integers N and X — the size of the array and the element to search for. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, ..., AN — the elements of the array.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing "YES" if X appears in the array, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case: 3 is present in the array, so output YES. Second test case: 10 is absent, so output NO.
The single element equals X, therefore YES.
7 does not appear in the array, so the answer is NO.

hidden.label: stress case 3
hidden.expectedOutput:
YES
NO
NO
NO
NO
NO
NO
NO
NO
NO

solution.approach: For each test case we simply scan the array once and check whether any element equals X. As soon as we find a match we can stop scanning, but even a full scan is O(N) which is optimal because we must look at each element in the worst case. Using BufferedReader and StringTokenizer ensures fast I/O for large inputs.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the input storage.

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws IOException{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());for(int t=0;t<T;t++){StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int N=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int X=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());boolean found=false;for(int i=0;i<N;i++){int val=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());if(val==X)found=true;}sb.append(found?"YES":"NO");if(t<T-1)sb.append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
